title: strychnos schultesiana
intro: Strychnos schultesiana, also known as the "guiana cursed tree" or "seeds of death", is a species of flowering plant in the family Loganiaceae, native to the Guiana Shield in South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. The health benefits of Strychnos schultesiana include its potential to reduce inflammation and pain, as well as its ability to act as an antimicrobial agent. In herbal preparations, the plant's seeds are often used to make medicinal teas, tinctures, and powders, although caution is advised due to the plant's toxicity.
